title: Agent harness skill
description: Schema for a single machine-readable workflow skill (in this repository, agent_skills/skills/). A skill is the agent-facing, source-traceable description of one documented workflow. It must be derived from the local repository; commands, tools, parameters, and databases are never invented. This schema documents the contract and supports optional structural validation; the Python hooks perform their own lightweight checks and do not require a JSON Schema validator.

name.description: Stable snake_case identifier, unique within the pack.

domain.description: The domain/category this workflow belongs to. In this repository, a One Health domain, e.g. 'Environmental Metagenomics', 'Clinical Isolates & Plasmid Profiling'.

description.description: What the workflow does and when to route to it.

supported_input_types.description: Input file types, e.g. pod5, fast5, fastq, fastq.gz, bam, fasta, vcf, paired_end_fastq.

tools.description: Tools the workflow invokes, with version pins from the repository environment files where available.

required_databases.description: Reference databases required. May be empty for workflows that need none.

parameters.description: Declared parameters. Only these may be used by command_builder; defaults must be repository-documented.

pre_hooks.description: Preflight checks to run before building commands, referencing functions in hooks/preflight.py.

command_templates.description: Ordered command templates, extracted verbatim from source_files. Use {placeholder} for declared parameters.

source.description: Repo-relative file the command was taken from.

post_hooks.description: Parsers to run on outputs, referencing functions in hooks/parsers.py.

validation_hooks.description: Sanity checks to run, referencing functions in hooks/validation.py.

caveats.description: Limitations and the standing note that LLM outputs are suggestions, not biological/clinical/regulatory/diagnostic conclusions.

source_files.description: Repo-relative paths this skill is derived from. Required for traceability.

external_references.description: Upstream/external URLs used only for comparison and completeness.

external_reference_notes.description: How upstream comparison relates to local behavior. Local repository remains the source of truth.

needs_review.description: true (or a list of specific notes) when repository-derived behavior is ambiguous, partial, or conflicts with upstream.
